title: derris brevipes var. coriacea
intro: Derris brevipes var. coriacea, also known as the Coriaceous Derris, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a natural insecticide and antifungal agent. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in rotenone, a toxic compound that has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Derris brevipes var. coriacea include decoctions and infusions made from its leaves and roots, which can be used to treat a range of health problems, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity.
